TextENGNew: One of the roads leading to Yagouchi was shunned by every villager.
Since it was a high-quality quarry, Yagouchi was frequented by stonemasons. However, they would always go out of their way to take a detour there, no matter how much of a hurry they were in.
As proof, beyond the small roadside shrine marking the entrance, the ground that had once been a road was now choked with weeds.
Though both paths must have been paved at the same time, they had since diverged into stark contrast.
``--Beyond this point, none shall trespass.``
Those ink letters on the worn-out roadside sign had been withered by the elements, and were barely visible at this point.
...Nevertheless, the ``curse`` that laid down this road was so feared that even village children brimming with curiosity still faithfully abided by that warning.
``Onigafuchi``.
That was what the villagers called the swamp hidden behind thick, overgrown grass.
It was said there was a hole in the swamp that links to another dimension connected to Hell, and that demons would occasionally crawl out of there to attack and kidnap villagers.
(...What an absurd story.)
I heaved an exasperated sigh, then stepped over the rope lined with holy paper that cordoned off the entrance of the road to keep people away.
The dewy grass that covered the ground was difficult to tread with sandals.
...Indeed, the rumors started when a cruelly mangled corpse had been found at this swamp, likely the victim of a beast or bandit.
Thoughtless remarks being spread turned that into a local superstition, and was established as if it were the truth.

All it would have taken was a little suspicion or investigation to reveal that the incident was the work of natural forces, but unfortunately no one had attempted to take the first step, likely induced by their insular climate and disposition.
(Although... I guess that makes things convenient for me right now.)
I muttered that to myself as I tugged at the cloak that concealed me from head to toe, then pulled it off.
I'd come this way by hiding myself in the shadows under the cover of evening, but now that I'd made it this far, it seemed I wouldn't be encountering anyone, fortunately.
The settlement vanished from view after I walked for a while.
Then I got off the road and cut across a ruined field. Afterwards, I went down a slope by a dilapidated house and reached the swamp.
...It truly had been a long time since I'd been here.
I approached the swamp while making my way through the thick stench of grass.
Ever since Ryuun-Ohc... Ryuuou had ceased functioning, the purification system had also gone offline, causing sediment to build up in the swamp and turn the water muddy.
And sluggish ripples spread across the now ugly dark surface of the water every time the wind blew.
(The entrance to Hell.@k@vS16/45/DS43071310. ...It's true that if you fell in here, there'd be no coming back.)
I took off the hood covering my head and kneeled before leaning forward and gazing into the swamp.
There, I faintly saw the familiar reflection of my face, hair... and horns.
I could see them clearly even through my crumpled and disheveled hair.
I gently touched them, as I unconsciously bit my lip at the displeasure and pain welling in my heart.
...These horns proved that I was me.
They were a symbol of loathsome fetters that reminded me of how we were sent to this world and ordered to fulfill our duty in this distant homeland.
If it weren't for these...
If I only I didn't have these... I could close my eyes, shut my ears, and pretend none of it had ever happened to begin with.
Then I could attain a gentle, lovely, and peaceful life that I could devote myself to.
I'd be lying if I said I wouldn't have any regrets... There had still been some meager happiness to be found amidst all that.
(But even so...)
I chose this way. That was an undeniable fact... as well as a sign of my unwavering will.
(This is it.@k@vS16/45/DS43071313. I'll put an end to it now...)
I closed my eyes and took a quiet breath.
Next, I opened my eyes in tandem with that breath, then muttered words from the former Ryuun homeworld as I gently stroked the surface of the swamp.
At that moment...
There was a blinding flash of light, and the surface of the water split in two.
A sword stained dark brown rose from the bottom of the darkness in that gap... and slowly settled into my hand.
``After I cease operations, part of the Lynos' functions will remain.@k@vS16/00/DS43050326. Should you ever need it, this sword will respond to your summons.``
``...But if possible, I pray that it never comes to that.``
``Ryuuou.@k@vS16/45/DS43071315. ...No, Mother.@k@vS16/45/DS43071316. It would seem I truly am in need of your help.``
After cutting the mouth of the sheath to check its sharpness, I returned the blade to its scabbard with a comforting grinding sound. I tied it at my hip, then looked up.
The eastern skies were turning indigo, and the edges of the mountains were gradually dyed black.
And when I looked to the west, I saw the exceptionally brilliant shine of a star not hidden by the deep vermillion dusk.
``The Evening Star.@k@vS16/45/DS43071318. ...I believe that is what they call it...``
I smiled bitterly at an irrational sentimentality brought on by the star's light, as I noticed I was unconsciously grabbing something at my chest, and opened my hand.
The red jewel on my necklace glittered. It must have fallen out when I bent over.
``...Red looks good on you, Hanyuu.``
Those words played back in my head.
``His`` kind and gentle smile came to mind, then vanished...
It all started a few days ago...
``...You're leaving the village?!``
Though I knew it was imprudent, I couldn't help but chuckle at his predictable reaction.
...Riku and I were all alone in the living room after cleaning up dinner and putting the sleeping baby to bed.
The hearth in the center of the room crackled as the firewood burned.
Riku held boiling water and a chartula on the other side of the flickering flame as he stared at me in shock.
``Why?@k@vS16/46/DS43020705. Please, explain your reasons to me!``
``Ahaha... My reasons, you say?@k@vS16/45/DS43071320. You should know that better than anyone else, should you not?``
``It seems one has appeared in the village.@k@vS16/45/DS43071322. ...A ``demon``, that is.``
``...Yes.@k@vS16/46/DS43020708. I've heard the same.``
Riku hung his head in resignation.
...It had happened four days ago.
The corpse of a stonemason was found on the shore of ``Onigafuchi Swamp`` in the outskirts of the village, brutally torn limb from limb.
He had gone missing while heading home from the Yagouchi quarry dead drunk, and rumor had it he'd taken the forbidden road by mistake and ended up falling victim to the ``curse``.
``I... I wasn't trying to keep it a secret from you.@k@vS16/46/DS43020710. It's just that the details are still vague, so I was thinking of telling you once we knew more...``
``Is that not the definition of a secret?``
Riku hung his head dejectedly... When I saw that, I felt I'd done enough and laid down my arms.
``No, I'm sorry, I went too far.@k@vS16/45/DS43071325. ...I understand how you feel.@k@vS16/45/DS43071326. You just didn't want me taking any risks, isn't that right?``

``...Consider me selfish if you may.@k@vS16/46/DS43020713. But it's no longer just yourself on the line here.@k@vS16/46/DS43020714. If something were to happen to you when you faced off against a demon, then I would...!``

``It's all right.@k@vS16/45/DS43071328. ...Or so I wish I could say, but there's no guarantee, is there?@k@vS16/45/DS43071329. There have been more than a few cases of would-be demon hunters... ending up the hunted instead.``
``Hanyuu...``
``However, if I don't go...@k@vS16/45/DS43071331. The ``demon's`` hunting instincts will certainly be awakened, leading to casualties.``
``It won't be that difficult of a fight if it's right after the awakening, but once it has fully fused it will take considerable strength not just to defeat it, but even damage it.``
No matter how far I prodded... in the end, I was a ``woman``. So long as I was physically inferior to men, timing and circumstances were essential for survival.
``But...@k@vS16/46/DS43020717. S-still...!@k@vS16/46/DS43020718. What does that have to do with you leaving the village?``
``It seems there are rumors floating around the village.@k@vS16/45/DS43071334. ...Rumors that the foreign woman living in Furude Shrine may be the avatar of a ``demon``.``
Riku's eyes and mouth opened wide... and then he awkwardly looked away.
He truly was such a poor liar.
(But... that's what I love about him.)
As I thought that to myself, the phrase ``love is blind`` came to mind, and I snorted.
Perhaps Riku thought I was messing with him when he saw that, as he pounded the floor.
``This isn't funny, Hanyuu!@k@vS16/46/DS43020721. And there are limits to how rude unfounded rumors like that can be!``
``I know.@k@vS16/45/DS43071337. But I believe it is true that everyone suspected that the day before last.``
``Yes.@k@vS16/45/DS43071339. Whether for good... or for ill.``
Two nights ago, I headed to the village assembly hall to bring something to Riku... only to hear him roaring in a rare display of anger from outside the door.
It was then that I learned there were rumors that I wore my hood to hide demon horns...
Of course, I didn't remember anyone apart from Riku and Shino seeing what was under my hood.
So it was nothing more than speculation, so Riku's excuse that it was used to hide grievous scars on my head was much more persuasive.
...Or rather, perhaps it was more accurate to say they were so overwhelmed by the normally docile Riku's rare display of harsh scolding that not even the elders could say anything back.

``Please stop this!@k@vS16/46/DS43020725. What gall have you to speak such unfounded conjecture?!!@k@vS16/46/DS43020726. Are you all saying that you've believed my wife to be a murderous criminal all this time?!``
``N-no, that's not...``
``And besides, it's only a small number of people spreading those rumors. We...``

``Still, there are things that should be said and things that should be left unsaid.@k@vS16/00/DS43030186. At the very least, I don't recall making friends with an inhuman woman that could be called a demon.``
``...You all have the right to speak, but consider a slight against her to be an insult against Riku and myself.``
Thankfully, Shino, who was standing in for the mayor, also interceded on my behalf and put in a good word for me. As a result, it was no coincidence that no further argument on the subject continued.
Even so... that didn't mean their suspicions were cleared. If the demon infestation continued, they would surely ask me to prove it.
And once my horns were revealed, no amount of covering from Riku and Shino would be able to clear their suspicions...
``...Still, there's no need for you to go.@k@vS16/46/DS43020728. I swear I'll persuade everyone again.``

``Even the villagers know what you're normally like, so I'm sure they will understand.@k@vS16/46/DS43020730. So Hanyuu... please trust me and reconsider.@k@vS16/46/DS43020731. Shino-san will surely help as well.``
Naturally. I'd never doubted Riku and Shino, even once.
Even so...
``Thank you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071342. ...But Riku.@k@vS16/45/DS43071343. What about Ouka... our daughter?``
``Children are pure... and therefore cruel.``
Just as Riku said, perhaps the adults would accept it... even if they wouldn't be fully satisfied.
But... what about the children?

They would take their family's irresponsible gossiping as fact. And since their thoughts and empathy weren't fully developed... they would direct their harsh words not at me or Riku, but at someone more harmless and defenseless... like Ouka, right?
I had no idea what sort of treatment they'd put her through.

But it was clear that it'd prove an unnecessary obstacle to her healthy growth, and even though they were children, she would face obstacles that she couldn't avoid or resolve, even with adult intervention.
``B-but...!@k@vS16/46/DS43020734. ...Ouka needs her mother...``
``Yes, I know.@k@vS16/45/DS43071346. ...So don't get the wrong idea, Riku.@k@vS16/45/DS43071347. That is exactly why I am going.``
``Hanyuu...?``
``I can't openly search for the ``demon`` without temporarily leaving the village.@k@vS16/45/DS43071349. ...So I will return once I have truly rid the village of this demon.``
``...How long will that take?``
I thought it over, ultimately settling on an answer that wasn't a lie, but still extremely optimistic.

``They day after I put an end to it all.@k@vS16/45/DS43071351. The search for the ``demon`` itself is the most time consuming part, but I do not believe even that will be long.@k@vS16/45/DS43071352. I'm sure of it...``
I smiled as cheerfully as possible at Riku to reassure him.
Even so, Riku remained silent. He kept taking an invisible teacup and setting it down to ease his nerves, and kept sighing as he considered my words.
(...This is a complete reversal of the old days.)
I used to be the one who was quiet all the time... Riku would probably get mad if I said that, though.
Even so, I believed I more than understood how he felt, even if he didn't put it into words.
Crackle...
The mountain of firewood in the hearth fell apart, and the fire spread to the ash below.
The two of us would go to bed once the fire went completely out... but Riku threw in a log meant for tomorrow morning in there, and we silently watched as the fire soon roared back to life.
``...Hanyuu.``
After a brief silence between the two of us...
...Riku suddenly broke that silence first.
``Please, promise me.@k@vS16/46/DS43020740. ...That you'll be sure to come back.``
I was about to say I'd be fine without really thinking about it, but swallowed my words when I saw the uncharacteristically serious gaze in his eyes.
Riku sidled up to me and firmly took my hand.
``...You... no, the person you've become has a home to return to.@k@vS16/46/DS43020742. No matter what happens, please do not forget that under any circumstances, if nothing else.``

``It has worried me for a long time now.@k@vS16/46/DS43020744. ...You get this rather fickle look on your face and stare off into the distance from time to time.@k@vS16/46/DS43020745. With cold eyes, as if you're looking at everything philosophically... no, with resignation.``
``Is... that right?@k@vS16/45/DS43071358. That's not...``
For a moment, I was about to deny Riku's concerns, but...
``...Yes.@k@vS16/45/DS43071360. Perhaps you are right...``
I smiled bitterly in self-deprecation when I found an epiphany in a corner of my mind.
``Until I came to this land and met you... I feel like all I ever thought about was my death.@k@vS16/45/DS43071362. As in, how exactly would I die.``
I visited a lot of places over a long period of time.
As I searched for many brethren and Grifys, and found myself in many life and death ``hunts``...
...I unconsciously began to wonder what sort of death would eventually claim me.
``Riku.@k@vS16/45/DS43071364. I believe it is natural in this world for your kind's bodies to age as you grow old, mature mentally, and ultimately die.``
``Yes... But what of it?``
``But it is the other way around for us.@k@vS16/45/DS43071366. In exchange for our bodies never aging, our minds retrogress...``

Our kind, who were known as high-order molecular lifeforms, did not physically age. This was because our lives were preserved not through cellular metabolism, but by the mutual interlocking and reconstitution of molecules.
On the other hand... our minds and thoughts grew simplified as a backlash to this optimization process.
It was what was called retrogression... though it may be more accurate to call it ``infantilization``.
In accordance with that, orders sent to body systems necessary for keeping us alive start getting simplified or omitted... and as a result, our molecular bonds grow fragile and eventually collapse.

That state is referred to as a ``free body``... but in this land, it's probably easier to understand by calling it a ``ghost``. It's a state where we lose the ability to shift from our astral bodies to our physical bodies.
Once that happens, we become invisible to lifeforms and those who possess physical bodies, who lose their ability to see, speak, and touch us.
And then we drift forever in some space, maintaining our primitive consciousness.

...However, according to what Ryuuou told me, there are rare exceptions where it is possible to attempt to share abilities and memories by ``sympathizing`` with the lifeform they are using as a medium.
But unfortunately... there's no one to confirm that with, so it's impossible to know.
``...Please stop.``

``I don't want to hear about any of that.@k@vS16/46/DS43020749. Death will come for me too someday, make no mistake.@k@vS16/46/DS43020750. But you will live.@k@vS16/46/DS43020751. I do not want to think about... what sort of death will be waiting for you.``
``...No, you have it wrong, Riku.``
I apologized for my insufficient explanation as I continued.

``Death comes for all living things. They are two sides of the same coin.@k@vS16/45/DS43071370. ...But because I'd seen too much of one side of that coin... I had forgotten how sacred the other side was.``
I learned through living with Riku just how delightful it is to live with someone else.
That is why I came to see the weight of death in a new light... and in exchange, discovered what a virtue life is.
That was why I wanted to protect it. Why I didn't want to lose it.
One could say that was how I, who had only been conscious of myself up until then, had discovered my heart.
That was what I wanted to convey...
``...Hanyuu.``
``So... I swear I will be back.@k@vS16/45/DS43071372. Because you are my home.``
And with those words...
I took off a necklace I had been wearing, and hung it around Riku's neck.
A bright and transparent crystal hung from a cord not of this world, containing faint light within.
``...What is this?``
``If anything happens while I am gone...@k@vS16/45/DS43071374. your voice can reach me through this.@k@vS16/45/DS43071375. I'm sure I will be back soon, but please use this in an emergency.``
Though I said that...
There was a higher probability of an emergency happening to me. Perhaps this crystal would become his memento of me.
Stop. Don't think about any of that right now.
This is it... This will mark my final mission as a Jedha.
So I swear I'll come back... to Riku and Ouka...
``What is it... huh?``
...I finally couldn't hold myself back.
I wrapped my arms around Riku's shoulders, drew in close, and buried my face in his chest.
Riku froze up in surprise for a moment... but it wasn't long before he gently embraced me.
``...Hanyuu.``
``Don't think of me as cold...@k@vS16/45/DS43071378. Even I... Even I...!``
``Yes... I know.@k@vS16/46/DS43020757. That is why I will wait for you.@k@vS16/46/DS43020758. Forever, if I have to...``
And then...
I stayed in Riku's arms until dawn, feeling his warmth the whole time...
...A vast number of silent husks littered the earth.
A smell like rusted iron enshrouded the air. Thick, cloudy liquids melded with the dirt to become mud, and even more juices from the bodies accumulated to form rivers.
The moonlight illuminated the remains of pure pandemonium.
It was a sight so merciless and gruesome that it was enough to make Hell seem like paradise in comparison.
Gwooooooh...!!
Legs as thick as logs stomped with earth-rumbling growls.
It crushed the guts spewing from the stomachs that had been split like pomegranates, and burst them open like the blooming of red flowers.
There was a ceaseless wet sound like a gushing fountain.
It was the sound of fresh blood sprouting from the neck of what had been the leader, kneeling motionless next to it.
There was nothing above it as though it'd been torn off, and the faint piece of white that poked out from the stump stood out as it was dyed a dull dark red.
``Eeek... eeeek...!!``
Two men slumped on the ground shrieked shrilly, clinging to their broken swords as they faced off against it.
With no chance of escape, yet having lost the will to fight back, they shuddered at the presence of the ``death`` approaching them and shivered as they shot looks pleading for mercy at it.
A group of night bandits posing as demons had attacked and killed villagers. With the villagers having been completely evacuated, they had planned to raid the empty village and steal valuables.
However... what they found waiting for them there was a real ``demon``.
Those ruffians were regretting and repenting for their actions over and over in the face of these reparations far too cruel for merely reaping what they sowed.
But the humans of this land didn't have the power to turn back time, so all they could do was despair at the reality before them, and loathe the majestic ``god`` who had established this causality.
The men, their teeth chattering out of sync, tried their best to scramble to their feet and get as far away from the monster as possible, even though their will to stand was shattered.
They desperately looked around them for help or an escape route, but their comrades had all been reduced to chunks of meat, and the roads were dead ends of abandoned houses and waterways.
Gaaghhhhhhh!
The ``demon`` opened its red mouth as it bared its fangs, then swung down its arm on the man on the left with a roar.
There was a dull thud and a faint scream, followed by the man with a massive gash gaping from his neck to his side.
``H-hyaaaaaaaaaah?!``
Blood splattered all over the man on the right, and he let out a half-crazed scream the moment he saw the red painting his body.
...But a second later, his face was grotesquely distorted, and his head soared through the air before crashing onto the ground.
Hhzzzh... hhhzzzzhh...!
The monster's throat echoed with an ominous breath akin to a growl.
The area was completely silent. Not even the insects were singing.
Only this ``demon`` wriggled in that dark world.
Gwooooh... gwooooooooh!!
A roar that seemed to tear the air apart with all its force rang out, and the birds that were resting in the trees nearby fell to the ground, too frozen with unprecedented fear to fly away.
The ``demon`` cruelly crushed them, taking step after step as the sensation of further blood and corpses made its heart shake.
...It could hear them.
The terrified screams of humans.
Voices calling for salvation, for forgiveness, praying to a god that wasn't even here.
Unfortunately for it, the residents had already evacuated in fear of the bandits, leaving no more ``prey`` behind.
...However, fresh and juicy humans were huddled in fear in the settlement down the road, waiting for the nightmare to continue.
What fools.
Even if they hide, we can tell where they are by perceiving their presences alone.
We weren't generous nor malicious enough to show tomorrow to those cowards who shivered in fear and holed themselves up without trying to carve their own paths.
That's right, that's the difference between the hunters and the hunted.
That was a just privilege permitted to us strong against the chosen weak.
In contrast, these residents are miserable, pathetic little creatures, crawling on the ground with tears in their eyes, begging for forgiveness while waiting for the disaster to pass by.
Tearing apart weaklings like those was no longer a ``hunt`` but a one-sided ``slaughter``, but at least they were useful for quenching our thirst.
And we waited.
For the time when prey worthy of the title game arrived, one whom we could trade wits and all our might with.
For the appearance of they who would spark the flame of light in us and make us tremble with the joy of awakening our instincts.
I had been waiting and hoping for that moment... for a very long time.
And this was the time... where I would sever these abominable chains and take my fate into my own hands...!
...They're close.
I felt a presence.
It seems that the bait had served their purpose well, even if they were fools...!
I heard a metallic hum...
``...Just as I thought.@k@vS16/45/DS43071380. You cannot fight blood, huh...?``
I heard a clear voice as cold as ice behind me.
The moment I turned around... she threw off her cloak, and drew her sword, its tip gleaming as she pointed it at me.
The wind howled.
Amidst the dance of flaxen hair, the hood covering her head was blown off by the breeze... clearly revealing two horns faintly shining in the moonlight.
And her red eyes that shone brightly even in the darkness cut through the night with a sharpness that clearly surpassed humans.
``...So, you are here, brethren. No...``
Now you are our natural enemy...
The Putus, those who had inherited the blood and soul of the Ryuun, who existed to hunt the Grifys and teach them death.
And... my most formidable rival!!
The area was surrounded by fire, perhaps ignited by the flames of the bandits' torches... The scorching heat blowing through the air caressed my cheeks and blew my hair up.
I'd known it was extremely likely... that the culprit wasn't a demon.
Because of their strong presence, demonified Ryuun detect nearby brethren and make their appearance known.
Since even a Putus such as I hadn't sensed anyone, I was sure that it had been the work of miscreants not of this region who had made use of the curse's name.
Even so... I let them run free to use them as bait.
For demonified Grifys to be lured by their moderate hostility and bloodlust.

When that happened, that prey with blackened hearts would be overwhelmed by the sheer difference in power, be slayed when their souls were painted white with fear and powerlessness, and their guts lovingly eaten.
There was no profound reason for it. The logical answer was that it was merely to make themselves known.
And they kept seeking indiscriminate massacre to sate those shallow desires of theirs, and continued living off bloodshed and tragedy.
...Which made this band of foolish bandits the perfect bait for demons.
And there was one more thing...
The reason they were trying so hard to make themselves known was because they sought a battle with us Putus.
Grifys bear hostility towards Putus from the moment their instincts awaken.
Not due to any self-defense instincts or hatred of their own kind, but because they simply want a fight.
Just like how I once pondered my own death... they too sought to discover the nature of death.
And what they want isn't prey who'll go down without a fight, but an opponent with which they can battle to the death.
In other words, our relationship isn't that of hunter and hunted. We are beings created by a bloody causality where we are drawn to each other in search of our own reason for existing.
It truly was extremely revolting. However... I could sense an impulse buried deep within me trembling with joy.
...Put another way, it was the stirring of the hunter blood sleeping in my instincts, greedily seething in search of prey.
(...Guess I can't fight blood either, huh?)
Right... Just like them, we Putus had also inherited hunter's blood.
No one could stop me from immersing in that pleasure, and I had no intention of stopping here and now, anyway.
Gwooooh... Gwoooooh...!
A roar echoed out... Truly a beast.
When I saw this ugly, despicable creature, my reasoning and lenience as a human fizzed out without a trace... and at the same time, the urge to kill rapidly swelled within me.
``...Three whole years.@k@vS16/45/DS43071383. You're the first one to remain in hiding until just before the marker wore off.``
I took a step forward.
I watched the monster's actions carefully without letting my guard down a second, while slowly but surely closing the gap with my tiptoes.
The faint moonlight illuminated the ugly, hairy muscles all over its arms and legs, as well as its sharp fangs and claws.
It looked down on me with wide eyes burning like crimson flames as it spewed lukewarm breath from its mouth, split all the way to its ears.
However... it had no horns. That was the feature of a demonified Grifys.
``...How ironic.``
Because I had horns that not even these monsters possessed, I was called a ``demon`` and not allowed to live as a human.
And the Grifys would use these horns as their mark -- baring their fangs and launching their assault.
In a sense, we were both beings branded as 'demons' against our will.
changeReason: Old translation says 'demons with opposing intentions,' implying the two had conflicting goals; new correctly translates 意思に反して as 'against our will,' meaning they were both unwillingly branded as demons.

TextENGNew: Even so... I would lament no more. And I intended to make this the final act of this farce.
For the ones waiting for my return, and for the future I would share with him -- I would end everything, here and now!
``Look upon me -- Grifys!!``
A moment later...
Two pairs of eyes shining scarlet like rubies in the darkness belonging to the ``demon`` who stood at twice my height, glaring down at me.
The sky and the ground were inverted in the monster's widened eyes.
It panted madly.
The moment the demon gasped in fear and faltered... I kicked off the earth and lunged right toward its chest.
Gaghhhhhhhhhhhh!
The demon swung its thick arms down at me half a moment later.
I kept charging despite that, raised my sword the moment he invaded my space, leapt forward with one big step... and swung at him with the force of that momentum!
``Daaaaaaaaaaah!!!``
Gshhhhhhh! Bshhhhhhh!
The demon's steel-like claws tore my right sleeve, but I dodged in the nick of time, sliced forward, and dug the tip of my sword into his side.
Then I jumped off the back of the demon's hand buried in the ground, as I sliced from hip to shoulder without the slightest hesitation.
``Deryaaaaaaaaaaah!!!``
Gwoooooooooh!!
Blood splattered, and the demon staggered back while letting out an agonized howl.
I tore off my right sleeve as I caught my breath, waiting for the right moment to swiftly jump back.
Then I looked at the demon. A deep straight cut was carved down his chest, gushing with blood.
However... the demon suddenly roared as if shaking off something, and grotesquely colored flesh instantly started rising like bubbles from its wound.
The blood then stopped, and the wound was sealed up in a twisted way.
``So you can regenerate, huh?@k@vS16/45/DS43071389. You've already reached terminal symptoms...``
I was considering just taking him prisoner if he could be treated, even if it would be harder to do so... but it seemed I didn't have much choice.
There was no other way but to completely erase his existence.
``...Listen, my brethren.``

I mentally apologized to Riku as I tore off my remaining left sleeve in one go and threw it away. Then I moved my left foot forward and stood with my sword at the ready as I quietly focused my mind on a single point.

``O descendant of those who abandoned their homeland, broke the horns that symbolized our race, and sought to live in a new land.@k@vS16/45/DS43071392. You have committed no sin, and I possess no right to punish you.``

``...However, to break the laws of the elders and infringe on the principles of this land is a foolish and shallow act that spits on the pride of my race and the feelings of our brethren in this land.``
And then...
I slowly pointed my lighter left arm at the demon, pulled back my right elbow like I were drawing a bow, and held my sword at level.
I placed the sword's bared tip on my left palm.
Next, I ran the gleaming sword across my upper arm, gushing with blood from the fresh wound as it speckled my sword in crimson.
It was the Turuy Galkia pose... the Twin Earth pose.
A refined one-hit kill thrust technique used to compensate for the disparity in physical strength and stamina against a demonified Grifys, which could deal fatal blows even when weakened.
The ``Ryuuou`` is normally a single edged sword, but it changes shape when you cast the incantation, and branching blades thrust out from the left and right to cut through the body's internal tissue.
Thus, with just a single stab, it severs the heart's four blood vessels, and sends a Putus's blood into their vitals, which is a lethal poison to a Grifys, destroying their body from the inside out.
...I have slain many of my race with this technique.
``I'll ask you once.@k@vS16/45/DS43071395. ...Do you choose the future, present...@k@vS16/45/DS43071396. or the inauspicious past?``
Gwoooooooooh!
The demon roared and started charging at me in lieu of an answer!
``...Then die!@k@vS16/45/DS43071398. There'll be no future for you!!``
I lowered my hips at the same time, as if lying in wait.
Then, using the open gap between two of his left fingers as my target, I located his heart and organs, and filled my right hand with strength.
The demon closed the gap between us with an earth-shattering tremor, raising his gleaming claws.
The air shook, and even time froze from his overwhelming bloodlust and destructive impulses.
...But on the other hand, I cleared and honed my mind, felt my enemy's blood and mind converging on a single point... and saw it clearly.
...This thrust would put an end to it all.
I took a breath in... and out. The moment my tension left with my breath, I put all my strength into the tip of my sword, and thrust right at the demon's vital!
``Taaaaaaah... Huh?!``
Just then, I hesitated.
My thrust went off-course and missed the target, merely slicing through the air.
That was my strongest and sole technique that I had never missed with once.
The price for missing... was leaving myself wide open.
Goaaaaaaaaahh!!
``...Kahaah?!``
The demon's slightly delayed strike hit true, and I was knocked a short distance away.
I was too stunned by the unexpected turn of events to brace myself, and so I couldn't breathe for a second after hitting the ground.
``Gaha... cough...! Cough...!``
I coughed violently as I held my side and stood up.
...Since it was his arm, not claw, that hit me, I'd avoided bleeding. But I still nearly passed out from the splitting pain, though I grit my teeth to endure it.
No...
That sort of pain no longer mattered...
``Aah... aaaah...!``
I lifted my head.
I looked at the demon's chest while praying that I was mistaken about what I'd glimpsed for an instant... only a mere instant.
``It can't be...!``
The clouds parted that moment, illuminating the area with moonlight, and then...
I saw it clearly with my eyes adjusted to the light.
That's... the necklace I gave Riku when I left...!
``Whyyyyyyyyy?!``
...It didn't take much thought to picture what had happened.
If he'd picked up from somewhere that the true identity of the fabled ``demon`` was actually some passing bandits...
He'd likely go to collect evidence of that to prove my innocence.
But he failed... and they attacked him to silence him.
Then what if, at that time, in order to survive... in order to fight... he'd awakened to his ``demonified`` instincts that lay dormant in the people of this land...?
``Aaah... uwaaah...!``
My teeth chattered, and my vision narrowed as if it were growing distant.
It couldn't be... This had to be a nightmare.
I admit... that when Riku and I met and we were first attracted to each other... I suspected the possibility.
That the reason we could be so open with each other might have something to do with my brethren's factor.
That was why I'd had him take that medicine so often it even made him suspicious, and checked his blood each time.
The result... was always negative.
It's true that as a resident of this land, he may have possessed something latent similar to the Grifys factor, but it wasn't enough to make him go berserk or mutate.
...Besides, I spent so much time with him, yet I noticed absolutely nothing.
I'd never felt that, not even during the countless times we'd slept in our bedroom. Not once!
As a result, I was relieved and didn't consider him a target I needed to keep my eye on... So why?!
Gwaaaaaaaaagh!!
The demon took advantage of my confusion to instantly close the gap and attack.
Then he swung his arm with all his might and thrust at me with his tearing claws.
``Ghhhh?!``
I instinctively raised my sword and managed to parry the blow just in the nick of time.
But that parry left me off guard for just a second, giving the demon time to roundhouse kick me in the side.
Thanks to our difference in physique working in his favor, I violently slammed into the ground and rolled across it.
I desperately got up, but the shock of that head-on attack still lingered, and I immediately fell limply to my knees.
What's more... I dropped my sword when I got blown away, and now the demon had grabbed it and was pointing its sharp edge my way...!
I instinctively gasped from the danger of the situation, but I never took my eyes off the demon, catching my breath to match its every move.
He seemed confident in his victory now that he'd taken my only weapon... but on the other hand, carrying an unfamiliar object only hindered his own movements.
I suddenly noticed an odd sensation on top of my tongue. What I'd thought was some gravel I'd gotten in my mouth when I rolled across the ground... was actually my tooth.
I finally realized I was bleeding from the mouth. A fishy stench rose from inside my throat.
That sensation... slowly but surely calmed my instincts.
Don't panic.
Don't be afraid.
If you keep your mind calm, the tide of battle will flow in your favor.
And the moment I suddenly remembered those words someone from my homeland had told me...
(...Here he comes!!)
The demon held my sword in both hands and swung it down as if he were trying to slice my skull open.
I stared straight down at its uneven trajectory... and caught the blade between my palms the moment it was a hair's breadth away from my forehead.
Gwoooooh!!
Though I'd stopped it, the demon filled the sword with more strength.
Every time, the tip of the blade slid down my palms and pierced my skin, blood from the wound trickling down the sword.
The blood passed through the sword's guard.
It oozed out from the handle and stained the demon's hands... burning him.
Gyaaaaaaooooooooooooooh!!
A disgusting stench emanated, like acid searing flesh, and white steam blew from the demon's hands.
He immediately let go at the unexpected pain, and I seized the chance to back off and spit the tooth in my mouth right at the demon's eye.
Gwoooooooh!!
The surprise attack hit him right in the eye, causing him to partially rotate the sword from the recoil, allowing me the opportunity to grab the handle.
Then I flashed the blade using the recoil from my twisted upper half, and sliced through his breastplate with a reverse shoulder slash.
Gaaaaaghhhhhhh!!
The demon screamed loud enough to tear his throat apart, held its wound, and backed off.
As the sword had Putus blood on it, steam rose from the wound as it burned, then chunks of flesh rotted away and fell to the ground with a charred stench.
``Haah... haah... haah...!``
I panted and caught my breath as the demon glared at me with hatred.
My whole body was covered in sweat, mud, and blood, and was almost at its limit... but the confusion left my mind, and my bewilderment changed to intense hatred, and I seethed.
``I know... I know what you are...!``
That's right.
Despite being a Grifys, it had stolen my sword, attacked orderly and consistently, and possessed unusual intelligence...
I gasped when I finally realized what it was... and grit my teeth when I understood what had been feeling so off for a while now.
``You... You're that Putus from back then, aren't you...?``
Right... This presence, this sensation... there's no doubt about it!!
Though it was extremely rare, it was possible for a Putus who'd lost their original physical body to keep living on as a free body.
And afterwards, it was possible for it to possess a different body... a human... and live on, like a Grifys.
He was a Putus who'd abandoned being a Putus and had obtained power greater than a Grifys... In other words, a true ``demon``...?!
``...THAT'S RIGHT...``
And then...
``...I am a former Putus... just like you. The one you slayed over 20 years ago...``
A repulsive smile rose to his ugly face... as he laughed.

``Why...?@k@vS16/45/DS43071417. Why did you possess Riku?@k@vS16/45/DS43071418. Why did you get him caught up in all of this when there was no benefit to you, and he was completely unrelated to begin with?!``
No, the bigger question was... why had a Putus ``demonified`` just like a Grifys, and attacked villages and natives?!
What reason did he have for not only abandoning our original goal of observing Grifys and stopping any who went berserk, but doing the very thing we had sworn to stop?!
``...I grew tired of it.``

``I faced endlessly continuing days of missions, and Grifys who never went extinct no matter how many I hunted.@k@vS16/00/VT4D_oni1025. That was still fine back when I felt excited to fight to the death and could get intoxicated off the smell of flesh and blood.``
``...But with the Grifys being mentally and physically inferior to us, it was hardly a contest.@k@vS16/00/VT4D_oni1027. Thus... I desired an opponent who could match my strength.``
``To fight Putus, men and women alike, to savor excitement and pleasure I'd never felt before in those battles.``
``...Though I was unexpectedly delayed because you, hailed as the strongest of our brethren... went straight for me before my powers had fully manifested...``

``...But if my abilities had fully awakened, I clearly should have won.@k@vS16/00/VT4D_oni1031. To prove that... and to rid myself of the humiliation I'd suffered at your hands... I sought a host...``
``Then I recovered from the wounds you gave me and gained the power to move as a free body three years ago... and discovered this village.``
``I desired a host... that would bring you the most anguish, rage, and despair when you pointed your sword at it... and who would quell my grudge and my hatred...``
``And that was...``
``This man you saved back then... and fell in love with...!!``
``What... was... that?``
I shivered.
My whole body violently trembled from head to toe, and I could stop it.
I remembered... I could clearly remember now!
He was definitely that scum who'd gotten Grifys involved in violence along with staining his own hands in slaughter... the one who'd nearly destroyed Onigafuchi Village!
I'd thought I defeated him for sure back then.
But he'd kept on living as a free body even after losing his flesh... maintained by his hatred and grudge that desired to defeat me.
And... in the end...
He'd gotten Riku caught up in this for his twisted pleasure... all for that stupid reason...!
``...Even you, of all people, will falter at the prospect of pointing a blade at the man you love...!``
``Wha... aah... aaaah...!``
``...How about it, kinslayer? Will you attempt to defeat me with that sword...? After all, if you kill me... he dies too...!``

Kill him? He who had nothing to do with this? He was a truly kind soul... who had shown me my greatest happiness... He was the most important and beloved person in my life... and I'd have to kill him...?!
``...You bastaaaaaaaard!!!``
Unable to hold back my surge of rage, I kicked off the ground with a bloodcurdling scream, erased all the pain in my body from my mind, and slashed at the scum before me.
My wild swings were easy to dodge and cut nothing but air multiple times, but my rage demanded his flesh and blood, so I kept up the assault.
``...I'll kill you!@k@vS16/45/DS43071425. I'll kill you, I'll kill you!!@k@vS16/45/DS43071426. How dare you... How dare you do that to my Rikuuuuuuuu!!``
``Hahahahahaha, yes, more! Hate me more! That is my peace! The words of congratulation that celebrate my victory in taking my rightful fate! Hahahahahaha!!``
``Uwaaaaaaaaaaah!!@k@vS16/45/DS43071428. Aaah! Haah!@k@vS16/45/DS43071429. Gaaaaaaaaagh!!!``
``Hahahahahaha!!``

``I'll kill you!@k@|@y@vS16/45/DS43071431. I'll kill you!@k@|@y@vS16/45/DS43071432. I'll kill you, I'll kill you, I'll kill you, I'll kill youuuuuuuuuu!!@k@|@y@vS16/45/DS43071433. I swear... you won't get away with thiiiiiis!!!``
And then...
I swung, slashed, thrust, and rotated my sword at the demon as though I'd lost my mind.
But in contrast to my raging emotions, my body was rapidly exhausting, slowing my actions.
They were so unsightly and pitiful that I couldn't tell if they were even attacks anymore... or mere child's play.
The demon swung at my neck right then.
I quickly parried with my sword, but it was blown away by that unavoidable gale, and stuck itself into the ground by a tree not too far from me.
I rose my upper body and started to get up... only for my knees to collapse on the spot.
I desperately mustered my willpower and looked up... to find the mocking smile of the demon looking down at me.
I couldn't move anymore.
I couldn't stand.
I couldn't fight fate... even if it had been cruelly twisted.
(...Oh, I see...)
This... could be my death.
I had constantly wondered when and how I'd meet my end... and it had finally come for me.
And if I died here...
Would my physical body be destroyed, reducing me to a free body just like this demon... or would I simply be destroyed altogether?
Either way... I no longer had any way out. And now that I'd lost my place to return... I no longer had the strength to stand.
...Really...?
Is this really... the end I wanted?
And... above all else...
Does he... want... to die here with me...?
``...Die, Putus woman! The wheels of fate are mine to command... as I will...!``
The demon said that...
And slowly raised his arm.
When I looked up at that, I faintly noticed something out of the corner of my eye.
It was the necklace's red jewel, shining in the moonlight.
There was a dull, metallic thud.
The earth cracked, and there was a war cry loud enough to split the sky.
The insects and forest creatures who'd forgotten to even make a sound...
Timidly poked their heads out from the leaves to see the outcome of the confrontation...
``...Gaagh? Gagh, gagggh...?!``
The demon's strike...
Had charged forward to crush and destroy my body and soul.
It was to be the finishing blow on an opponent at death's door.
But right before it could hit...
I stopped that claw with my blade.
``...You're wrong...!``
I endured the pain in my creaking body and pushed back the demon's arm, mere centimeters away from me.
``...You... no, the person you've become has a home to return to.@k@vS16/46/DS43020760. No matter what happens, please do not forget that under any circumstances, if nothing else.``
``...Please stop.@k@vS16/46/DS43020762. I don't want to hear about any of that.@k@vS16/46/DS43020763. Not about what kind of death will be waiting for you...``
Riku's words from back then played back in my mind.
They made me happy.
So... I knew what I had to do.
Screw fate.
My own death?! Don't screw with me!!
There is only one thing I believe in this world... So I just have to believe in that!!
``Riku... Riku would never want that!@k@vS16/45/DS43071441. He'd wish for me to live, not die!@k@vS16/45/DS43071442. That's the essence of the Riku Furude I loved!!``
``Ghh... D-damn you...!!``
``Like hell I'll die...!@k@vS16/45/DS43071444. Like hell I'll break my promise to Riku... and submit to youuuuuuuu!!``
``Wh-what nonsense is that...! Why won't you just dieeeeeee?!!``
``...Don't make me laugh.``
``...You're the one who refuses to die!``
``Wha?! ...Gwaaaaaagh!!``
A splash of blood danced through the air.
The demon screamed in agony as he covered his eyes and thew his head back.
Vast blood poured out from between his fingers, and he stumbled around helplessly in intense pain as he lost his sight.
I swiped the blood off the claws sticking out of my left fingers, and bared the giant fangs pouring out of my mouth.
Then I glared at him with red eyes lighter than scarlet.
``...Don't you go thinking that the clan's claws are yours alone... filth!``
``Gaggh... Y-you... Impossible, have you demonified as well?!``
I turned to the stunned demon... and flashed him a cruel smile.
The ``claw blades``... a demon's power that was unconsciously invoked by rage and bloodlust.
I could obtain power greater than a ``demonified`` member of my clan by fully unleashing it, but since it would destroy my sense of reason if I used it too long, it truly was a double-edged sword.
Fortunately, the demon couldn't see it with his eyes crushed... No, perhaps he was unlucky.
For if he could see this face, he'd have reached the horrifying realization that I wasn't some opponent to be taken lightly...!
``I won't eat you.``
``...But I will never forgive you...!``
``Curse your shallowness and inability to reflect on your cowardice as you die, ``failure``!!``
I then retracted my ``claw blades``, took my sword and thrust it in my left hand, then faced the demon with one foot forward...
I ran the blade up my left arm and donned the Turuy Galkia stance.

The demon miserably swung his arms, perhaps having sensed that and desperately trying to escape from the danger, but his claws cut through nothing but air, and not even their lingering gales reached me.
No... nothing reached me anymore.
No sounds. No presences.
I simply stood with my sword at the ready in a dark, quiet void.
(...I'm sorry, Riku.)
I couldn't save you.
I can't smile, get mad... or spend time with you ever again.
That thought alone... caused me to burst into tears, with no signs of stopping...
But... please understand. At least you, if no one else...
What I can do now...
And what you'd surely wish for...
Is for me to simply believe in that... and kill you.
Perhaps I'm simply deluding myself.
If I'm wrong... then I'll regret my foolishness and injustice for the rest of my life, and may very well never smile again.
Even so, it's fine. I will now... become a demon.
Then I will burden myself with all the sins and live as a ``wrathful god`` that will be a target for all fear and hatred... until the moment my life comes to an end...!
``...Ghh... d-damn you! I-if it's come to this, then I'll just abandon this body, possess a new host, and take you d... Wha?!``
That moment.
Gwaaaagh... Gwaaaaaaaaagh!!
The demon suddenly stood up with a roar and faced me head on.
I instinctively braced myself, but he bent his arms that were broken like toys, grabbed the necklace hanging by his chest and tore it off... and trembled as he held it out to me...
``...Wh-why?! Why can't I separate from this body?! L-let me go, you bastard!!``
(...It's all right, Hanyuu.)
In my mind...
I heard the faint echo of the voice of the man I loved with all my heart.
It was that kind, gentle voice... that clear voice that would always warm my heart just by hearing it... his voice...!
I'd... resigned myself to never hearing it again.
I'd believed his soul to have been ground to dust when the Putus took over his body, and grieved.
But... but Riku was...!
Calling out to me at the very end... all for me... to bring my heart back from despair...!!

(...No matter what anyone calls you...@k@vS16/46/DS43020766. You are my beloved Hanyuu.@k@vS16/46/DS43020767. Never forget that.@k@vS16/46/DS43020768. No matter what happens... make sure you never forget that...!)
``...Aah... aaaah... aaaaaah...!!``
His kind words... tore my heart to shreds.
Just when I'd thought I had no more tears to shed, even more welled out of me, restoring patches of light to the world that had been stained black.
...If this were any other time...
If we were at Furude Shrine and Riku said those words with a smile, just like always...!
I would have puffed my cheeks with a hint of embarrassment... and headbutted him in the chest.
Then he would gently wrap his arms around me... and I could lean on him, closing my eyes and giving myself into peace of mind.
``There was a world... like that.@k@vS16/45/DS43071463. We really did that... in a time so recently I can remember it like it was yesterday!!!``
My shout was empty.
That memory was just sad.
It brought tears and pain to my body and heart.
``Uuu... uwaaah...!@k@vS16/45/DS43071465. ...Uwaaaaaaaah!!``
My throat and chest... burned in pain, as if they'd been gouged out.
I swear...
That no matter what happens...
I will never, ever... forget this pain and suffering!
Never... Never!!
``Riku... I loved you...!@k@vS16/45/DS43071467. I truly... loved you more than anyone in the world!!``
And then...
I swallowed my tears, took a breath, and pointed my gleaming sword tip at his heart.
(Farewell, Riku...!!)
I thought I could hear a happy tone in Riku's voice when I muttered that.
I gathered every last thought into a single point of focus... and with the very last of my strength, I kicked off the earth.
``N-nooooooooo--!!``
``Uuu... uwaaaaaaaaaaah--!!!``
``...Hanyuu.@k@vS16/46/DS43020768_2. Ouka... I leave her in your hands.``
``Ryuuou, Type Two... Triple Prong, releaseeee!!``
